Sprint 1 - MVP 2.title: Add notifications
Sprint 1 - MVP 2.description:
Currently, users do not receive any feedback or alerts for important actions such as successful operations, errors, or system events. This leads to poor user experience and lack of clarity during interactions.

The notifications system will provide real-time feedback to users through UI messages (e.g., success, error, info notifications). This will improve usability and help users understand the outcome of their actions.

This task involves implementing a notification component on the frontend, supporting different types of messages, and ensuring proper handling of user interactions and system responses.

Acceptance Criteria:

* User receives notification on key actions (e.g., add to cart, payment success/failure)
* Notifications support different types (success, error, info)
* Notifications automatically disappear after a few seconds
* Multiple notifications can be handled without UI issues
* Proper error messages are displayed for failed operations
* UI should be clean and non-intrusive

Priority: Medium
Type: Task

Sprint 1 - MVP 2.title: Performance testing
Sprint 1 - MVP 2.description: Perform performance testing to analyze system behaviour under load and identify bottlenecks for optimization.

Sprint 1 - MVP 2.title: Fix checkout crash
Sprint 1 - MVP 2.description:
Users are experiencing a crash during the checkout process when attempting to complete a purchase. This issue is impacting the user experience and blocking successful transactions.

The root cause appears to be related to improper handling of edge cases in the payment or cart validation logic. The system does not gracefully handle invalid or missing input, leading to application failure.

This task involves identifying the exact cause of the crash, implementing proper error handling, and ensuring the checkout flow works reliably under all valid and invalid scenarios.

Acceptance Criteria:

* Checkout process should not crash under any condition
* Proper error handling must be implemented for invalid inputs
* Users should see a meaningful error message instead of application failure
* Payment flow should complete successfully for valid inputs
* Unit tests should be added to cover edge cases

Priority: High
Type: Bug
